levels.body: “Mien” can be understood as a facial expression or bearing of the face that exposes one’s mood or one’s true character ("Mien, *n*. (1)", Sense 1.).
worksCited: “Mien, *n*. (1).” *Oxford English Dictionary*, Oxford UP, December 2025, doi.org/10.1093/OED/1019859598. 18 Mar. 2026.

levels.body: The raven is portrayed as exhibiting a stance typically associated with humans, personifying the [[raven]]. This personification of the raven is further enhanced a few lines below when the raven is characterized as having a “stern [[decorum]]” (line 44). This is also in agreement with a more elaborate understanding of “mien” as the “personification of a quality” ("Mien, *n*. (1)" Sense 2.).
worksCited: “Mien, *n*. (1).” *Oxford English Dictionary*, Oxford UP, December 2025, doi.org/10.1093/OED/1019859598. 18 Mar. 2026.
